warnings: A formal Model Risk Management framework must be implemented.

feasibility.summary: Catastrophic error consequences may make AI automation inadvisable.

warnings: CRITICAL MISMATCH: Supervised learning (Classification/Regression) is not possible with unlabeled data. A data annotation phase is a mandatory prerequisite.
STRATEGY SHIFT: With fully labeled data, this is a classification problem, which is more robust than unsupervised anomaly detection.
STRATEGY SHIFT: With partial labels, a semi-supervised approach is recommended for better accuracy than purely unsupervised methods.

STRATEGY NOTE: As the data is completely unlabeled, the only viable approach is Unsupervised Anomaly Detection. If any labeled examples exist, even a few, re-assess using the 'Partially Labeled' option for a more powerful semi-supervised approach.
TECHNOLOGY NOTE: For structured data, consider if traditional database queries (SQL, etc.) can solve the problem before implementing a more complex RAG/vector search system.
CRITICAL BUDGET MISMATCH: Budget is insufficient for a full production deployment.
RECOMMENDATION: Re-scope to PoC or MVP, or increase budget significantly. Production AI systems typically require $150k-$500k+.

feasibility.summary: Project scope and budget are fundamentally misaligned. Not viable as stated.

warnings: BUDGET WARNING: Budget is tight for MVP scope. Industry benchmarks suggest $25k-$120k for a viable MVP.
RECOMMENDATION: Prioritize a narrow feature set and leverage managed services/APIs to reduce development costs.

feasibility.summary: MVP may be achievable with a tightly scoped feature set, but budget leaves no room for iteration.

warnings: RISK: Budget may be insufficient for a production system with no internal expertise.
Consider MVP scope or an increased budget for a comprehensive handover.
CRITICAL EXPERTISE MISMATCH: No internal AI expertise but expecting complex maintenance.
This combination is not feasible without significant training or hiring.

techProfile.Strategy: Transfer Learning / Fine-tuning pre-trained models is the ONLY viable approach.
techProfile.DataAugmentation: Data augmentation and synthetic data generation are strongly recommended to expand effective dataset size.

warnings: DATA LIMITATION: The dataset is small. Transfer learning from pre-trained models is the recommended approach.
Pre-trained models are available for most common CV and NLP tasks and can achieve strong results even with limited fine-tuning data.
avoidTech: Training new model architectures from scratch.

feasibility.summary: Limited data severely constrains approach options.

warnings: CRITICAL PRIVACY RISK: Using PII with Generative AI poses extreme privacy and legal risks.
Data anonymization/pseudonymization is MANDATORY before any LLM interaction.
Third-party model APIs may retain sensitive data.
avoidTech: Sending raw PII to third-party model APIs.

warnings: CRITICAL COMPLIANCE RISK: Generative AI in regulated environments requires extensive controls.
Hallucinations and unpredictable outputs pose major compliance threats.
May require regulatory approval before deployment.
HIGH RISK COMBINATION: Agentic AI with significant error impact requires extensive safety guardrails.
Mandatory human-in-the-loop oversight, comprehensive logging, and strict action boundaries are required. Deploy in supervised mode with escalation protocols.

feasibility.summary: This combination requires heavy investment in safety infrastructure and human oversight.

warnings: IMPOSSIBLE REQUIREMENTS: Extreme accuracy (>99%) with limited data is typically not achievable.
Reconsider if this accuracy level is truly necessary.
BUDGET MISMATCH: High availability requirements cannot be met with this budget.
HA infrastructure TCO (including personnel, monitoring, and redundancy) typically ranges from $115k-$300k+ annually.
EXPERTISE GAP: Streaming data requires specialized expertise not available internally.
Streaming systems are significantly more complex than batch processing.
SCOPE-BUDGET MISMATCH: Multi-lingual support requires additional resources for data sourcing, model evaluation, and quality assurance across languages.
With modern multilingual models and efficient fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA), each additional language typically adds 15-30% to development effort, primarily in data curation and evaluation.
